name: Hilo
meaning: The first sliver — beginning, the thread of the new month
best_for: Plant crops, start projects, light fishing
notes: Hilo means 'to twist' — the first thread of the new lunar month

name: Hoaka
meaning: The gleaming one — the crescent shows clearly
notes: Hoaka means to gleam or show

name: Kūkahi
meaning: Kū stands first — the god Kū arrives
notes: Three Kū nights begin. Associated with the god Kū.

name: Kūlua
meaning: Kū doubled — second night of Kū
notes: Second Kū night.

name: Kūkolu
meaning: Kū three — near the first quarter
notes: Third Kū night. Moon at first quarter.

name: Kūpau
meaning: Kū exhausted — end of the Kū period
notes: Closes the Kū sequence.

name: ʻOlekukahi
meaning: First ʻOle — barren night, rest from labor
notes: ʻOle means barren. Unproductive for fishing and farming.

name: ʻOlekulua
meaning: Second ʻOle — continue rest
notes: Second barren night.

name: ʻOlekukolu
meaning: Third ʻOle — barren period continues
notes: Third barren night.

name: ʻOlepau
meaning: ʻOle complete — end of barren nights
notes: Last ʻOle night.

name: Huna
meaning: Hidden — the moon's power builds beneath the surface
notes: Huna means hidden or secret.

name: Mohalu
meaning: Unfolding — the moon opens like a blossom
notes: Mohalu means to spread out. Very favorable night.

name: Hua
meaning: Fruit / Egg — full ripening approaches
best_for: Best fishing nights begin. Root crop planting.
notes: Hua means egg or fruit.

name: Akua
meaning: The god — the moon belongs to the gods
notes: Associated with Lono and divine presence.

name: Hoku
meaning: Star / Full — maximum light, the celebrated night
best_for: Night fishing at its best. Navigation. Ceremony.
notes: Most celebrated lunar night. Surfers gathered. Voyagers launched.

name: Māhealani
meaning: Still full and radiant — the moon in its fullness
notes: Named for the wife of a legendary chief.

name: Kulu
meaning: Dripping / Falling — the moon begins its descent
best_for: Good fishing, beginning of waning cycle
notes: Kulu means to drip or fall.

name: Lāʻaukapau
meaning: Strong wood — solid and reliable
notes: Good for durable work. Moon still bright.

name: Lāʻaukūkahi
meaning: First Lāʻau — wood nights begin
notes: Lāʻau nights associated with trees and timber.

name: Lāʻaukulua
meaning: Second Lāʻau
notes: Second Lāʻau night.

name: ʻOle
meaning: Barren again — second ʻOle period
taboos: Limited fishing. Rest from heavy labor.
notes: Second barren sequence. Moon past last quarter.

name: Kāloakūkahi
meaning: First Kāloa — the moon moves toward darkness
notes: Kāloa nights associated with Kāne in some traditions.

name: Kāloakulua
meaning: Second Kāloa
notes: Second Kāloa. Moon rises later each night.

name: Kāloakukolu
meaning: Third Kāloa
notes: Third Kāloa.

name: Kāloakupau
meaning: Kāloa complete
notes: End of Kāloa sequence.

name: ʻOloli
meaning: Slender / Quiet — the thin waning crescent
notes: ʻOloli means slender. The moon wanes to a thin crescent.

name: Kāne
meaning: Kāne — god of fresh water and life
best_for: Sacred to Kāne. Fishing, healing, water ceremony.
notes: One of the most sacred nights. Favored departure night for ocean voyages.

name: Lono
meaning: Lono — god of agriculture and rain
best_for: Planting, rain prayers, new month preparation
notes: Dedicated to Lono — god of agriculture and Makahiki.

name: Mauli
meaning: Last breath — the moon nearly gone
best_for: Rest. Complete all work before the new month.
notes: Mauli means breath or life-force.

name: Muku
meaning: Cut off — the month ends, the moon is gone
best_for: Rest. Deep stillness before the new begins.
notes: Muku means severed. The moon is entirely dark. The cycle awaits renewal.
